title: 6 3 Quadric Surfaces
body:
6.3 QUADRIC SURFACES

When a standard quadric surface

┬▒ 2 ┬▒ 2 ┬▒ 2 =1 a b c is rotated in space mixed terms xy, xz and yz appear in the equation. It is possible to express the resulting quadratic form in matrix form xT Ax = 1 where A is a symmetric matrix. An analysis of the eigenvectors and eigenvalues of A will reveal both the geometry and the principal axes of the surface. x2 y 2 Рђб + 2 = 1 is an ellipse in R2 . (++) a2 b

x2 y 2 Рђб Рѕњ 2 = 1 is a hyperbola in R2 (+Рѕњ). a2 b

Рђб 2 + 2 + 2 = 1 is an ellipsoid in R3 (+ + +). a b c

Рђб + 2 Рѕњ 2 = 1 is a hyperboloid of one sheet in R3 (+ + Рѕњ). a2 b c Рѕњ 2 Рѕњ 2 = 1 is a hyperboloid of 2 sheets in R3 (+ Рѕњ Рѕњ). a2 b c

Consider the curve 2x2 Рѕњ 4xy + 5y 2 = 54. The presence of the mixed term xy indicates that this is a standard object (ellipse or hyperbola) which has been rotated to some degree so that its major and minor axes no longer point in the x and y directions. To understand the curve we need to apply a specific transformation that РђюunrotatesРђЮ the curve into standard form.

Our first step is to rewrite the equation in terms of matrices.  T    2 2 x 2 Рѕњ2 x Claim: 2x Рѕњ 4xy + 5y = 54 is equivalent to = 54 y Рѕњ2 5 y Proof:

Thus, we can express any quadratic curve in the form xT Ax where A is a symmetric matrix by construction. As we saw in the last lecture, this means that the eigenvectors of A will be orthogonal to one another. We can now calculate the complete set of eigenvalues and eigenvectors of A.

So the eigenvalues of A are 1 and 6 with associated unit eigenvectors №БФ 2 №БХ №БФ 1 №БХ Рѕџ РѕњРѕџ v╠ѓ1 = №БГ 15 №БИ and v╠ѓ2 = №БГ 2 5 №БИ №Бг №Би №Бг №Би Рѕџ Рѕџ 5 5 These directions form the principal axes of the curve. If we define a new new orthogonal coordinate system {X, Y } aligned with the vectors v╠ѓ1 and v╠ѓ2 , then the curve has the simplest algebraic form. In this coordinate system the equation of the curve is

1X 2 + 6Y 2 = 54.

Note the appearance of the eigenvalues! We can now identify the curve as an ellipse. Rewriting the equation as  2  2 X Y Рѕџ + =1 54 3 we see that the closest points to the origin is ┬▒3 units in the Y direction. In the {x, y} coordinate system these points are №БФ 3 №БХ №БФ 3 №БХ РѕњРѕџ +Рѕџ 3v╠ѓ2 = №БГ 5 №Би Рѕњ3v╠ѓ2 = №БГ 5 №Би 6 №БИ, 6 №БИ. №Бг №Бг +Рѕџ РѕњРѕџ 5 5

Example 3 Express the equation of the surface

x2 + 2y 2 + 2z 2 + 4xy Рѕњ 4xz + 6yz = 30

in terms of its principal axes X, Y and Z and hence determine the nature of the surface. Find an orthogonal matrix P that transforms the surface to its principal axes, №БФ №БХ №БФ №БХ x X №БГ y №БИ=P№БГ Y №БИ z Z

Deduce the shortest distance from the surface to the origin and the {x, y, z} coordinates of these closest point(s).
